name: GrainBuf
doc: Granular synthesis with sound stored in a buffer

argDocs.numChannels: the number of channels to output. If 1, mono is returned and pan is ignored.
argDocs.trigger: a kr or ar trigger to start a new grain. If ar, grains after the start of the synth are sample accurate.
argDocs.sndbuf: the buffer holding a mono audio signal. If using multi-channel files, use Buffer.readChannel.
argDocs.rate: the playback rate of the sampled sound
argDocs.pos: the playback position for the grain to start with (0 is beginning, 1 is end of file)
argDocs.interp: the interpolation method used for pitchshifting grains: 1 = no interpolation 2 = linear 4 = cubic interpolation (more computationally intensive)
argDocs.pan: Determines where to pan the output. If num-channels = 1, no panning is done; if num-channels = 2, panning is similar to Pan2; if num-channels > 2, pannins is the same as PanAz.
argDocs.envbufnum: the buffer number containing a singal to use for the grain envelope. -1 uses a built-in Hanning envelope.
argDocs.maxGrains: the maximum number of overlapping grains that can be used at a given time. This value is set at the UGens init time and can't be modified. This can be set lower for more efficient use of memory.

name: GrainFM
doc: Granular synthesis with frequency modulated sine tones

argDocs.numChannels: the number of channels to output. If 1, mono is returned and pan is ignored.
argDocs.trigger: a kr or ar trigger to start a new grain. If ar, grains after the start of the synth are sample accurate.
argDocs.carFreq: the frequency of the FM grain's carrier oscillator
argDocs.modFreq: the frequency of the FM grain's modulating oscillator
argDocs.pan: Determines where to pan the output. If num-channels = 1, no panning is done; if num-channels = 2, panning is similar to Pan2; if numChannels > 2, pannins is the same as PanAz.
argDocs.envbufnum: the buffer number containing a singal to use for the grain envelope. -1 uses a built-in Hanning envelope.
argDocs.maxGrains: the maximum number of overlapping grains that can be used at a given time. This value is set at the UGens init time and can't be modified. This can be set lower for more efficient use of memory.

argDocs.pan: Determines where to pan the output. If num-channels = 1, no panning is done; if num-channels = 2, panning is similar to Pan2; if num-channels > 2, pannins is the same as PanAz.

argDocs.maxGrains: the maximum number of overlapping grains that can be used at a given time. This value is set at the UGens init time and can't be modified. This can be set lower for more efficient use of memory.

argDocs.numChannels: the number of channels to output. If 1, mono is returned and pan is ignored.
argDocs.trigger: a kr or ar trigger to start a new grain. If ar, grains after the start of the synth are sample accurate.
argDocs.freq: the frequency of the grain's oscillator
argDocs.pan: Determines where to pan the output. If num-channels = 1, no panning is done; if num-channels = 2, panning is similar to Pan2; if numChannels > 2, pannins is the same as PanAz.

name: Warp1
doc: A granular time stretcher and pitchshifter. Inspired by Chad Kirby's SuperCollider2 Warp1 class, which was inspired by Richard Karpen's sndwarp for CSound.

argDocs.numChannels: the number of channels in the soundfile used in bufnum.
argDocs.bufnum: the buffer number of a mono soundfile.
argDocs.pointer: the position in the buffer. The value should be between 0 and 1, with 0 being the begining of the buffer, and 1 the end.
argDocs.freqScale: the amount of frequency shift. 1.0 is normal, 0.5 is one octave down, 2.0 is one octave up. Negative values play the soundfile backwards.
argDocs.windowSize: the size of each grain window.
argDocs.envbufnum: the buffer number containing a singal to use for the grain envelope. -1 uses a built-in Hanning envelope.
argDocs.windowRandRatio: the amount of randomness to the windowing function. Must be between 0 (no randomness) to 1.0 (probably to random actually)
argDocs.interp: the interpolation method used for pitchshifting grains. 1 = no interpolation. 2 = linear. 4 = cubic interpolation (more computationally intensive).
